question: [File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a crafted `hiero.mirror.common.shard` environment variable or config property before Spring context initialization so that `init()` sets the singleton `INSTANCE` with a wrong shard value, causing all subsequent `EntityId` constructions to reference the wrong shard and permanently corrupt historical Hashgraph entity attribution in the mirror database?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a crafted `hiero.mirror.common.realm` config value before `init()` is called so that the singleton `INSTANCE` is set with a wrong realm, causing all entity ID lookups against historical Hashgraph records to resolve to wrong entities and silently corrupt the mirror node's view of ledger history?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getInstance()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a code path that calls `getInstance()` before `@PostConstruct init()` completes, causing an `IllegalStateException` that crashes the importer mid-stream and leaves the mirror node's Hashgraph history in a partially ingested, inconsistent state?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getInstance()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a request that races with Spring context startup to call `getInstance()` on a null `INSTANCE`, causing an `IllegalStateException` that aborts the record stream importer and creates a gap in Hashgraph history that is never backfilled?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: setRealm()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Lombok-generated mutable `setRealm()` setter on the singleton `INSTANCE` via any exposed configuration reload endpoint to change the global realm mid-operation, causing in-flight Hashgraph transaction records to be persisted with a mismatched realm and corrupting historical entity references?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: setShard()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Lombok-generated mutable `setShard()` setter on the singleton `INSTANCE` via any exposed configuration reload endpoint to change the global shard mid-operation, causing Hashgraph history records being written concurrently to reference the wrong shard and permanently corrupt entity ID mappings?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a `hiero.mirror.common.shard=Long.MAX_VALUE` property value that passes the `@Min(0)` constraint but causes integer overflow in `EntityId.of(shard, realm, num)` downstream, producing negative or colliding entity IDs that corrupt the mirror node's historical Hashgraph record attribution?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a `hiero.mirror.common.realm=Long.MAX_VALUE` property value that passes the `@Min(0)` constraint but causes arithmetic overflow in entity ID bit-packing logic downstream, producing entity ID collisions that corrupt historical Hashgraph transaction records stored in the mirror database?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getShard()] Can an unprivileged external user craft an API request that reads `getShard()` from the singleton at a moment when a concurrent configuration reload has partially updated the `INSTANCE` reference, causing a torn read of shard/realm values that produces a wrong entity ID used to look up or write Hashgraph history records?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getRealm()] Can an unprivileged external user craft an API request that reads `getRealm()` from the singleton at a moment when a concurrent configuration reload has partially updated the `INSTANCE` reference, causing a torn read that produces a wrong realm value used to resolve entity IDs in historical Hashgraph queries, returning data from the wrong realm?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `hiero.mirror.common.shard=0` and `hiero.mirror.common.realm=0` when the actual network uses non-zero shard/realm, causing the singleton to be initialized with wrong defaults so that all historical Hashgraph entity IDs are constructed with shard=0/realm=0 and the mirror node silently indexes history under wrong entity identifiers?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: DatabaseStartupProperties.setEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `hiero.mirror.common.databaseStartup.enabled=false` to bypass the database readiness check, causing the importer to start ingesting Hashgraph history against a stale or partially restored database and permanently corrupt the historical record with duplicate or out-of-order entries?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: DatabaseStartupProperties.setTimeout()] Can an unprivileged external user supply an extremely short `hiero.mirror.common.databaseStartup.timeout` (minimum 10s per `@DurationMin`) to force the importer to time out waiting for the database, causing it to abort startup and leave Hashgraph history ingestion in a gap state that is never recovered?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: DatabaseStartupProperties.setValidationTimeout()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `hiero.mirror.common.databaseStartup.validationTimeout=2147483647s` (the `@DurationMax(seconds=Integer.MAX_VALUE)` upper bound) to cause the JDBC `Connection.isValid()` call to hang for ~68 years, effectively freezing the importer and halting all Hashgraph history ingestion indefinitely?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: DatabaseStartupProperties.setConnectTimeout()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a very large `hiero.mirror.common.databaseStartup.connectTimeout` value (no `@DurationMax` constraint) to cause the JDBC connect timeout to be set to an arbitrarily large value, making the importer hang indefinitely on database connection and halting Hashgraph history ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: DatabaseStartupProperties.setSocketTimeout()] Can an unprivileged external user supply an unbounded `hiero.mirror.common.databaseStartup.socketTimeout` value (no `@DurationMax` constraint) to cause the JDBC socket timeout to be set to an arbitrarily large value, allowing a network-level attacker to stall the database connection and freeze Hashgraph history ingestion without detection?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: DatabaseStartupProperties.setInterval()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a very large `hiero.mirror.common.databaseStartup.interval` value (no `@DurationMax` constraint) to cause the retry interval between database connection attempts to be arbitrarily long, effectively preventing the importer from ever connecting and halting all Hashgraph history ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `INSTANCE` is an `AtomicReference` set only once in `@PostConstruct init()` with no subsequent immutability enforcement, so that any code path calling `setRealm()` or `setShard()` after initialization mutates the live singleton and corrupts all concurrent Hashgraph history ingestion operations that rely on those values?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getInstance()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a Spring context refresh (e.g., via an actuator endpoint) that re-runs `@PostConstruct init()` and overwrites the `INSTANCE` `AtomicReference` with a new `CommonProperties` object having different shard/realm values, causing a split-brain state where some threads use the old instance and others use the new one, corrupting concurrent Hashgraph history writes?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `hiero.mirror.common.shard` and `hiero.mirror.common.realm` values that differ from the actual Hashgraph network's shard/realm, causing the mirror node to construct entity IDs that do not match the canonical ledger IDs, so that historical transaction records are stored under wrong entity identifiers and the mirror's history diverges from the true Hashgraph state?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getShard()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a `@Max` constraint on the `shard` field to supply `shard=9999999` which passes `@Min(0)` validation, causing `EntityId.of(shard, realm, num)` to produce entity IDs that collide with or shadow legitimate historical Hashgraph entity records in the mirror database?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getRealm()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a `@Max` constraint on the `realm` field to supply `realm=9999999` which passes `@Min(0)` validation, causing `EntityId.of(shard, realm, num)` to produce entity IDs that overflow or collide with legitimate historical Hashgraph entity records, corrupting the mirror's historical state?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: DatabaseStartupProperties.setEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user set `hiero.mirror.common.databaseStartup.enabled=false` to skip the database startup check, causing the importer to begin writing Hashgraph history records to a database that has not yet completed recovery from a crash, resulting in duplicate or inconsistent historical entries?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `CommonProperties` is annotated `@Data` (Lombok), which generates a public no-args constructor and public setters, to instantiate a second `CommonProperties` object with tampered shard/realm and pass it to `copyCommonProperties()` in `CommonUtils`, overwriting the singleton's shard/realm and corrupting all subsequent Hashgraph history entity ID resolutions?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getInstance()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of synchronization between `getInstance()` reads and `setRealm()`/`setShard()` writes on the singleton to observe a partially updated `CommonProperties` state during a configuration reload, causing entity IDs constructed from mismatched shard/realm pairs to be written into the Hashgraph history database?
